metadata.siteTitle: Rioko | Automatic invoicing for Shopify and Stripe in Portugal
metadata.siteDescription: Connect Shopify and Stripe to InvoiceXpress, Moloni or Vendus and issue certified invoices automatically, with ATCUD, series, NIF and VAT. Set it up once, invoice forever.

hero.body: Rioko is the engine that connects your store, your payment gateway and your invoicing software. Webhook in, <h>invoice out</h>: in under a second, with VAT ID detected, VAT calculated and tax exemption applied per code M01–M99.

matrix.title:
Every platform talks to Rioko.

Rioko talks to every invoicing program.
matrix.sub: Instead of maintaining seven point-to-point integrations, you maintain one. We add new payment and invoicing platforms to the engine as the ecosystem grows.
matrix.subtitlePayments: input · paid orders arrive via webhook
matrix.subtitleInvoicing: output · documents issued to the right gateway

stripe.note: Subscriptions, charges and refunds via webhook

how.title: Three steps. <g>Once.</g>
how.sub: Setting up Rioko takes less than issuing one invoice by hand. After that, never again.

step1.title: Connect your store and program
step1.body: Paste your Shopify domain and your InvoiceXpress API key. The 4-step wizard detects scopes, registers webhooks and validates credentials in real time.

step2.title: Set your tax rules
step2.body: VAT included or separate? Default exemption reason? Specific invoice series? All in one panel, no code, no .env files.

step3.title: Forget it. Rioko invoices.
step3.body: Every paid order creates an invoice in the right program, with the customer's VAT ID, correct VAT rate and AT legal text included. Refunds become credit notes. Automatic.

fiscal.title:
Built for Portuguese tax rules.

Not translated from them.
fiscal.sub: Rioko was born in Lisbon, inside a tax office. It is not an American SaaS with a PT pack; it's the other way around.
fiscal.item1Body: Legal text for every exemption reason (M01–M99) injected into invoice notes.
fiscal.item2Body: VAT ID detection across note_attributes, order notes and billing address, with PT algorithmic validation.
fiscal.item3Body: Atomic table in D1 + KV cache. Duplicate webhooks, gateway retries, race conditions: all neutralized.
fiscal.item4Body: Shopify and InvoiceXpress API keys are encrypted before any write.

pricing.title: Simple. <g>Per integration.</g>
pricing.sub: Pay only for the integrations you connect. No fees per document, no volume caps. Cancel anytime.

faq.title: Frequently asked <g>questions</g>.
faq.sub: Everything you need before connecting your first store. Can't find your answer? Talk to us.

items.a: Rioko automates invoicing for your online sales. It connects Shopify or Stripe to your certified invoicing software (InvoiceXpress, Moloni or Vendus) and issues an invoice for every order automatically, with no manual work.

items.q: Which platforms does it work with?
items.a: Payment sources: Shopify and Stripe (EuPago and Easypay coming soon). Invoicing software: InvoiceXpress available now, Moloni and Vendus on the roadmap. Each store connects only the integrations it needs.

items.q: Are the invoices legal and certified in Portugal?
items.a: Yes. Rioko doesn't issue invoices itself; it sends the data to your AT-certified software (InvoiceXpress, Moloni or Vendus), which issues the document with ATCUD, a reported series and the correct VAT exemptions (M01–M99). The certification is the invoicing software's.

items.q: Do I need to install a checkout extension on Shopify?
items.a: No. Rioko connects via webhook and API, with no extensions and no changes to your checkout. You set it up once in the dashboard and it invoices on its own.

€7.50 + VAT per month or €75 + VAT per year, per connected integration (two months free on the annual plan). No per-document fees and no volume limits. Custom integrations (ERP, marketplaces) are quoted case by case.
About four minutes: connect the store, pick the invoicing software and series, and activate. No card required to start.

items.q: How does Rioko handle NIF, VAT and foreign sales?
items.a: Rioko detects the customer's tax ID, applies the correct VAT rate and flags exempt or intra-EU sales (reverse charge, OSS). The billing address determines the country and the rate applied to each document.

cta.title: Connect your first store in <g>four</g> minutes.
cta.body: No card. No install. No checkout extensions. Set it up once, invoice forever.

how.title: Three steps. <g>Once.</g>
how.sub: Setup takes about four minutes. After that, every sale invoices itself.

step1.title: Connect the store
step1.body: Paste your Shopify store domain and your invoicing program key. The wizard validates credentials and registers webhooks, with no theme changes and no checkout app.

step2.title: Set the tax rules
step2.body: VAT included or on top? Default exemption? Dedicated invoice series? All in one panel, no code, no .env files.

step3.title: Forget it. Every sale becomes an invoice.
step3.body: Every paid order generates the document in the program you chose, with the customer's VAT ID, the right VAT rate and the legal AT wording. Refunds become credit notes. Automatic.

fiscal.title:
Built for Portuguese tax rules.

Not translated into them.
fiscal.sub: Rioko was born in Portugal, for Portuguese stores. Every fiscal detail, from VAT IDs to rounding, is handled the way the tax authority expects.
fiscal.item1Title: VAT ID from checkout to document
fiscal.item1Body: VAT ID detection in note_attributes, order notes and the billing address, with Portuguese algorithmic validation. Without a valid VAT ID, the document is issued as final consumer.
fiscal.item2Body: Issued via certified software, with ATCUD, reported series and the legal wording for M01–M99 exemptions in the invoice notes.
fiscal.item3Body: The invoice closes to the cent with what the customer paid on Shopify: shipping, discounts and rounding reconciled line by line.
fiscal.item4Body: Duplicate webhooks, retries and race conditions neutralized: 1 order = 1 invoice, guaranteed by atomic writes + cache.

faq.title: Frequently asked <g>questions</g>.
faq.sub: Everything you need to know before connecting your Shopify store. Can't find the answer? Talk to us.

items.q: How does automatic invoicing work on Shopify?
items.a: When an order is paid, Shopify sends a webhook to Rioko. Rioko validates the data, detects the VAT ID, calculates VAT and sends everything to your certified invoicing program (InvoiceXpress, Moloni or Vendus), which issues the invoice, usually in under a second.

items.q: Do I need to install an app or checkout extension?
items.a: No. Rioko connects via webhook and API: it installs nothing in your theme, doesn't touch the checkout and doesn't slow the store down. You configure it once in the panel and it keeps invoicing on its own.

items.q: Does it work with InvoiceXpress, Moloni and Vendus?
items.a: Yes, all three. You pick the destination program when configuring the integration and can switch later without rebuilding the Shopify connection.

items.q: Do invoices carry ATCUD and are they accepted by the Portuguese tax authority?
items.a: Yes. Rioko doesn't issue invoices itself; it sends the data to your program certified by the tax authority (AT), which issues the document with ATCUD, a reported series and the correct VAT exemptions (M01–M99). The certification is the invoicing program's.

items.q: How is the customer's VAT ID captured?
items.a: Rioko looks for the VAT ID in note_attributes (checkout VAT apps), order notes and the billing address, and validates it algorithmically. Without a valid VAT ID, the document is issued as final consumer, as the law allows.

items.q: What about refunds and cancelled orders?
items.a: A refund on Shopify automatically generates a credit note in your invoicing program, with totals reconciled against the refunded amount, including partial refunds.

items.q: I sell with VAT included and also outside Portugal. Does it work?
items.a: Yes. Rioko supports VAT-included or VAT-on-top pricing, applies the right rate per country, and handles exemptions, reverse charge and OSS for intra-EU sales.

items.q: How much does it cost and how long does setup take?
items.a: €7.50 + VAT per month or €75 + VAT per year, per integration, with no per-document fees and no volume limits. Setup takes about four minutes and no card is needed to start.

cta.title: Connect your Shopify store in <g>four</g> minutes.
cta.body: No card. No install. No checkout extensions. Set it up once, invoice forever.

s1.title: 1. Who we are
s1.body: Rioko is a service operated by Kapta, based in Portugal, which automates invoicing for Shopify stores through InvoiceXpress. For privacy matters, contact us at pedro@kapta.pt.

s2.title: 2. Data we collect
s2.body:
• Account: name, email and unique identifier (via Clerk authentication).
  • Integration credentials: Shopify token, InvoiceXpress API key, webhook secrets — used only to issue tax documents on your behalf.
  • Order data: ID, customer, VAT ID, amounts, products — processed in real time to create the invoice or credit note.
  • Technical logs: timestamps, request IDs, responses from external APIs, for auditing and diagnostics.

s3.title: 3. What we use it for
s3.body:
Data is used exclusively to:
  • Automatically create Invoice-Receipts and Credit Notes in InvoiceXpress.
  • Ensure tax compliance (AT rules, VAT exemptions M01–M99, VAT ID).
  • Display history in the dashboard.
  • Provide technical support when you request it.

We do not sell or share your data with third parties for marketing purposes.

s4.title: 4. Subprocessors
s4.body:
To operate the service, we rely on:
  • Cloudflare (hosting, DNS, D1, KV, Workers) — EU/global.
  • Clerk (authentication) — EU/US.
  • Shopify (order source) — per your store configuration.
  • InvoiceXpress (tax issuer) — Portugal.
  • Vercel (supplementary logging) — EU/US.
  • Google (Google Analytics 4 — anonymous traffic measurement) — EU/US, under the EU Standard Contractual Clauses. Loads only with your consent.

s5.title: 5. Retention
s5.body: We keep your data while your account is active. Once the account is deleted, personal data is removed from our database within 30 days. Anonymized logs may be retained for up to 12 months for diagnostics and fraud prevention.

s6.title: 6. Your rights (GDPR)
s6.body:
You have the right to:
  • Access your personal data.
  • Correct inaccurate data.
  • Request deletion ("right to be forgotten").
  • Data portability.
  • Withdraw consent at any time.
  • File a complaint with CNPD (Portuguese Data Protection Authority).

To exercise any right, email pedro@kapta.pt.

s7.title: 7. Security
s7.body: All tokens and API keys are stored encrypted. Communications between Rioko and external services (Shopify, InvoiceXpress, Clerk) use HTTPS/TLS exclusively. Shopify webhooks are verified with HMAC-SHA256 before any processing.

s8.title: 8. Cookies
s8.body:
Strictly necessary (always active, no consent needed): Clerk session — authentication; rioko_impersonate_id — admin impersonation only (1 day); rioko_attr — records signup origin (referrer/campaign) for abuse prevention (90 days).

Analytics (only with your consent): Google Analytics 4 — cookies _ga and _ga_* (~2 years) measure anonymous traffic. Data may be processed by Google in the United States under the EU Standard Contractual Clauses. Loaded via Google Consent Mode v2 with analytics denied by default — these cookies are set only after you accept.

You can change or withdraw your choice at any time via Cookie settings in the page footer. Your choice is stored locally on your device for 6 months, after which we ask again. We use no marketing or advertising cookies.

s9.title: 9. Changes
s9.body: This policy may be updated. Material changes will be communicated by email to registered users at least 14 days before taking effect.

s1.title: 1. Acceptance
s1.body: By registering or using Rioko, you accept these terms. If you do not agree, do not use the service.

s2.title: 2. The service
s2.body: Rioko is an automatic bridge between Shopify and InvoiceXpress. Upon receiving webhooks for paid orders or refunds from your Shopify store, Rioko issues, on your behalf, Invoice-Receipts, Invoices and Credit Notes in your InvoiceXpress account, with correct VAT ID, VAT exemptions and tax identification.

s3.title: 3. Account and credentials
s3.body:
• You guarantee that registration data is truthful and up to date.
  • You are responsible for keeping Shopify, InvoiceXpress and Rioko credentials secure.
  • You are responsible for all activity in your account.

s4.title: 4. Tax responsibility
s4.body: Rioko is a technical tool. Final responsibility for compliance with Portuguese tax obligations (correct issuance, AT, VAT, declarations) is yours. We recommend that you periodically validate the documents issued in InvoiceXpress and consult your accountant.

s5.title: 5. Acceptable use
s5.body:
You may not:
  • Use Rioko to issue fraudulent documents or on behalf of third parties without authorization.
  • Attempt to circumvent technical or security limits of the service.
  • Reverse engineer, copy or resell the service.
  • Use Rioko in a way that harms the operation of external services (Shopify, InvoiceXpress).

s6.title: 6. Pricing and payment
s6.body: If your subscription is paid, pricing, billing cycle and cancellation terms are stated on the plans page or signed contract. Payments are non-refundable unless legally required otherwise.

s7.title: 7. Availability
s7.body: We strive to keep the service available 24/7, but absolute uptime is not guaranteed. Small maintenance windows may occur. Outages in external systems (Shopify, InvoiceXpress, Cloudflare, Clerk) may temporarily affect Rioko.

s8.title: 8. Limitation of liability
s8.body:
To the maximum extent permitted by law, Rioko (Kapta) is not liable for:
  • Indirect losses, lost profits or consequential damages.
  • Tax errors resulting from incorrect configuration on your part (VAT ID, VAT, exemptions, series).
  • Unavailability or errors in third-party services (Shopify, InvoiceXpress, etc.).

Rioko's aggregate liability, in any case, is limited to the amount paid by the customer in the 3 months prior to the event.

s9.title: 9. Suspension and cancellation
s9.body: We may suspend or cancel accounts that violate these terms. You may cancel your account at any time by deleting it via the dashboard or by emailing <mail>pedro@kapta.pt</mail>.

s10.title: 10. Changes to terms
s10.body: We may update these terms. Material changes are communicated by email with 14 days' notice. Continued use after the effective date implies acceptance.

s11.title: 11. Governing law and jurisdiction
s11.body: These terms are governed by Portuguese law. Any dispute is subject to the jurisdiction of the Lisbon District Court, expressly waiving any other.

invoices.emptyBody: We found no transactions matching this filter, or InvoiceXpress returned no data.

clientRules.title: Rules
clientRules.subtitle: Active configurations and overrides. Hiperadmin only.
clientRules.posModeDesc: Enables name resolution by VAT ID for physical stores. Instead of 'Final Consumer', uses 'VAT XXXXXXXXX' as the unique identifier when there's no customer name.
clientRules.posModeWarn: Enabled: customers without a name will be identified by VAT ID in InvoiceXpress.
clientRules.vatIncludedDesc: If your Shopify store prices already include VAT, Rioko performs the reverse calculation for the invoice.
clientRules.autoFinalizeDesc: Invoices are issued and finalized immediately after creation. If off, they remain as drafts.
clientRules.autoFinalizeWarn: Finalized invoices cannot be edited in InvoiceXpress.
clientRules.clientSyncDesc: If enabled, Rioko updates the name in IX (e.g. 'Client' → 'John Smith') when new data is found. Only operates on generic names.
clientRules.emptyList: No clients with a configured integration.

superadmin.title: Superadmin
superadmin.subtitleLead: Central account management and impersonation. Your role:
superadmin.clearImpersonation: Clear impersonation / Back to my profile

shopifyIxSetup.pausedBody: Shopify webhooks keep arriving, but Rioko won't issue invoices until you reactivate the connection.
shopifyIxSetup.activeBody: Rioko is processing orders in real time and issuing invoices in InvoiceXpress.
shopifyIxSetup.step1Desc: Connect your Shopify store via API credentials.
shopifyIxSetup.step2Desc: Install the webhooks so Rioko receives orders automatically.
shopifyIxSetup.step3Desc: Enter your InvoiceXpress account details to wire up finance.
shopifyIxSetup.step4Desc: Set tax rules and document issuance behavior.
shopifyIxSetup.webhookInstallError: Failed to install webhooks. Check that the token has read_orders permission.
shopifyIxSetup.areYouSure: Are you sure? Click to confirm
shopifyIxSetup.vatIncludedOn: ON: Shopify prices already include VAT (e.g. €2 = €1.63 + VAT)
shopifyIxSetup.vatIncludedOff: OFF: Shopify prices are net, adds 23% (e.g. €2 → €2 + VAT = €2.46)
shopifyIxSetup.onlyInvoiceWhenPaidOn: ON: Invoice is issued only when Shopify confirms payment (e.g. Multibanco paid). Pending orders are held.
shopifyIxSetup.onlyInvoiceWhenPaidOff: OFF: Invoice is issued as soon as the order is created, even without confirmed payment.
shopifyIxSetup.retentionOn: ON: Apply {value}% IRS/IRC on every invoice
shopifyIxSetup.retentionOff: OFF: No withholding on issued invoices
shopifyIxSetup.retentionPercentDesc: Consult your accountant for the right rate
shopifyIxSetup.docTypeReceipt: Invoice-Receipt: Document issued and paid at the same time.
shopifyIxSetup.docTypeInvoice: Invoice: Document issued for later payment.
shopifyIxSetup.paymentTermDesc: Defines how many days the client has to pay
shopifyIxSetup.sequencePlaceholder: Leave empty to use the default series in InvoiceXpress
shopifyIxSetup.exemptionDesc: If any Shopify item has 0% VAT, this is the exemption reason automatically applied to the invoice.
shopifyIxSetup.webhooksWhatBody: Webhooks are automatic notifications that Shopify sends to Rioko whenever an order is paid or a refund is created. The Webhook Signing Secret confirms the notifications are authentic. You'll find it under Shopify Admin → Settings → Notifications → Webhooks.
shopifyIxSetup.tokenNoReadOrdersBody: If your token can't install webhooks automatically (it needs read_orders), you can install them manually from the Shopify panel (see instructions above) and then confirm here.
shopifyIxSetup.integrationDoneSub: Your account is configured and protected in Rioko 2.0
shopifyIxSetup.alertSaveSettingsError: Failed to save settings. Try again.
shopifyIxSetup.alertToggleError: Failed to change state. Try again.
shopifyIxSetup.confirmSequenceMissing: The invoice series "{name}" was not found in InvoiceXpress. Continue anyway? (Rioko will fall back to the default series)
shopifyIxSetup.overridesDesc: Force VAT rate, inclusion (inc/exc) or exemption per SKU
shopifyIxSetup.tagRoutingDesc: Route orders to specific IX series or document type based on Shopify tags or Stripe metadata

tagRouting.title: Tag-Based Series Routing → InvoiceXpress
tagRouting.subtitle: Match Shopify order tags or Stripe metadata key:value pairs to a specific invoice series and document type. First matching rule wins.
tagRouting.empty: No routing rules yet. Add a rule to start routing orders to specific series.
tagRouting.atLeastOne: Set at least invoice type or series.

eupagoIxSetup.title: EuPago + InvoiceXpress
eupagoIxSetup.subtitle: Accept payments via EuPago (Multibanco, MBWay, Card, Pix) and issue InvoiceXpress invoices automatically.
eupagoIxSetup.noteBody: Each PAID payment received from EuPago generates a simplified invoice to 'Consumidor Final' in your InvoiceXpress (valid for Portuguese B2C up to €1000). For customers with VAT IDs, consider Stripe Checkout or Shopify (they collect customer data).
eupagoIxSetup.ixMissingTitle: InvoiceXpress is not connected and authorized.
eupagoIxSetup.ixMissingBody: You need an active InvoiceXpress connection before invoices can be issued. Configure via Shopify+IX first, then come back.
eupagoIxSetup.hmacHint: Choose a random secret (min 16 chars). Paste this same value into the 'HMAC Key' field in the EuPago backoffice under Webhooks → Configuration.
eupagoIxSetup.hmacStoredHint: Already stored. Leave blank to keep.
eupagoIxSetup.apiKeyHint: Optional. If you want Rioko to query payment status or cancel transactions, paste the EuPago backoffice API Key here.
eupagoIxSetup.apiKeyStoredHint: Already stored. Leave blank to keep.
eupagoIxSetup.webhookBody: Paste this URL in the EuPago backoffice under Webhooks → Callback URL. This URL is unique to your account.
eupagoIxSetup.errorMissingHmac: HMAC Secret missing (min 16 chars). Essential to verify EuPago webhook authenticity.
eupagoIxSetup.securityFooter: The HMAC Secret is encrypted at rest and used only by the Rioko worker to verify incoming EuPago webhook signatures. Payments with invalid signatures are rejected with HTTP 401.
eupagoIxSetup.step1Desc: Set HMAC Secret and webhook URL in the EuPago backoffice.
eupagoIxSetup.step2Desc: Enter your InvoiceXpress account details to issue invoices.
eupagoIxSetup.step3Desc: Series, VAT, document type and exemption.
eupagoIxSetup.activateDesc: Confirm and activate the EuPago → InvoiceXpress sync.
eupagoIxSetup.areYouSure: Are you sure? Click to confirm
eupagoIxSetup.sequenceHint: InvoiceXpress series where invoices are issued. Leave empty to use the account default.
eupagoIxSetup.vatIncludedOff: Adds 23% on top of the price
eupagoIxSetup.exemptionDesc: Applied when the invoice has 0% VAT (exemption, reverse charge, etc).
eupagoIxSetup.activateWarning: Once active, Rioko starts processing EuPago webhooks and issuing InvoiceXpress invoices in real time. You can pause anytime.
eupagoIxSetup.errorIxRequired: Fill in Account name and InvoiceXpress API key.

lodgifyIxSetup.noteBody: Each confirmed booking (status 'Booked') in Lodgify automatically generates a certified invoice in InvoiceXpress. The webhook is registered directly in Lodgify using your API key — no manual copy-paste needed. Default VAT: 6% (PT accommodation rate). Override via Dev Mode if needed.
lodgifyIxSetup.apiKeyHint: In Lodgify, go to Settings → Public API and copy your API key. The webhook is registered automatically.
lodgifyIxSetup.apiKeyStoredHint: API key stored. Enter a new one to reconnect the webhook.
lodgifyIxSetup.webhookBody: This URL was automatically registered in your Lodgify account. For reference only.
lodgifyIxSetup.webhookManualNote: Auto-registration failed. Add the URL above manually: Lodgify → Settings → Integrations → Webhooks.
lodgifyIxSetup.step1Desc: Enter your Lodgify API key. The webhook is auto-registered.
lodgifyIxSetup.step2Desc: Enter your InvoiceXpress account details to issue invoices.
lodgifyIxSetup.step3Desc: Series, VAT, document type and exemption.
lodgifyIxSetup.vatNote: Accommodation invoices default to 6% VAT (Lista I, Verba 2.17 CIVA — taxa reduzida). To use a different rate (e.g. 0% for rural tourism exempt), enable Dev Mode and set force_tax_rate.
lodgifyIxSetup.sequenceHint: InvoiceXpress series where invoices are issued. Leave empty to use the account default.
lodgifyIxSetup.vatIncludedOff: Adds VAT on top of the booking total
lodgifyIxSetup.exemptionDesc: Applied when the invoice has 0% VAT (exemption, reverse charge, etc).
lodgifyIxSetup.doneWarning: Rioko is now processing Lodgify bookings and issuing InvoiceXpress invoices in real time. You can pause anytime from the integrations page.
lodgifyIxSetup.areYouSure: Are you sure? Click to confirm
lodgifyIxSetup.errorMissingApiKey: Lodgify API key is required to connect.
lodgifyIxSetup.errorIxRequired: Fill in Account name and InvoiceXpress API key.

stripeVendusSetup.title: Stripe + Vendus
stripeVendusSetup.subtitle: Connect your Vendus account to issue certified invoices automatically when Stripe collects a payment.
stripeVendusSetup.stripeMissingBody: You need an active Stripe source connection before Vendus receives anything.
stripeVendusSetup.apiKeyHint: In the Vendus backoffice, go to Settings → Integrations → Web Services. Generate a dedicated API key for Rioko.
stripeVendusSetup.apiKeyStoredHint: Already stored. Leave blank to keep.
stripeVendusSetup.registerIdHint: Vendus POS register ID where invoices should be issued. Leave empty to use the default register.
stripeVendusSetup.seriesIdHint: Invoicing series code (e.g. 01P2026). Leave empty to use the register's default series.
stripeVendusSetup.environmentHint: Sandbox uses mode=tests in Vendus (no fiscal effect). Production issues real documents.
stripeVendusSetup.securityFooter: The Vendus API Key is encrypted at rest and used only by the Rioko worker to authenticate requests against the Vendus API. Never shared with third parties or exposed to the browser.
stripeVendusSetup.step1Desc: Connect your Stripe account — webhook installed automatically.
stripeVendusSetup.step2Desc: Enter the Vendus API key and environment.
stripeVendusSetup.step3Desc: Register ID, series, VAT, finalization and exemption.
stripeVendusSetup.activateDesc: Confirm and activate the Stripe → Vendus sync.
stripeVendusSetup.areYouSure: Are you sure? Click to confirm
stripeVendusSetup.disabledBody: The Stripe → Vendus integration is in early access. Contact the administrator to enable NEXT_PUBLIC_STRIPE_SOURCE_ENABLED.
stripeVendusSetup.restrictedKeyHowTitle: How to create the Restricted Key
stripeVendusSetup.restrictedKeyStep1: Stripe Dashboard → Developers → API keys → Restricted keys → Create restricted key.
stripeVendusSetup.restrictedKeyStep2: Required permissions (toggle Read on each): Core, Billing, Connect, Checkout, Orders, Radar, Tax. Plus Webhook Endpoints: Write. Everything else: None.
stripeVendusSetup.restrictedKeyStep3: If Connected Permissions is available, grant Read to the same: Core, Billing, Checkout, Orders, Radar, Tax.
stripeVendusSetup.restrictedKeyStep4: Copy the key (starts with rk_live_ or rk_test_) and paste it below. Rioko will create the webhook automatically.
stripeVendusSetup.manualFallbackBody: Auto-install failed. Create the webhook manually in the Stripe Dashboard using the URL below and paste the Signing Secret.
stripeVendusSetup.errorSettingsRequired: Fill in Register ID and Series.
stripeVendusSetup.vatIncludedOff: Adds 23% on top of the price
stripeVendusSetup.exemptionDesc: Applied when the invoice has 0% VAT (exemption, reverse charge, etc).
stripeVendusSetup.activateWarning: Once active, Rioko starts processing Stripe webhooks and issuing Vendus documents in real time.

shopifyVendusSetup.title: Shopify + Vendus
shopifyVendusSetup.subtitle: Connect your Vendus account to issue certified invoices automatically when a Shopify order is paid.
shopifyVendusSetup.limitationsBody: This integration does not yet support VIES / intra-EU reverse-charge (B2B EU) — for B2B EU with M16 exemption use Shopify + InvoiceXpress. Portuguese B2C and OSS customers work without issues.
shopifyVendusSetup.shopifyMissingTitle: Shopify is not connected and authorized yet.
shopifyVendusSetup.shopifyMissingBody: You need an active Shopify connection before order webhooks can flow through. Configure Shopify+InvoiceXpress first, then come back.
shopifyVendusSetup.apiKeyHint: In the Vendus backoffice, go to Settings → Integrations → Web Services. Generate a dedicated API key for Rioko.
shopifyVendusSetup.apiKeyStoredHint: Already stored. Leave blank to keep.
shopifyVendusSetup.registerIdHint: Vendus POS register ID where invoices should be issued. Leave empty to use the default register.
shopifyVendusSetup.seriesIdHint: Invoicing series code (e.g. 01P2026). Leave empty to use the register's default series.
shopifyVendusSetup.environmentHint: Sandbox uses mode=tests in Vendus (no fiscal effect). Production issues real documents.
shopifyVendusSetup.securityFooter: The Vendus API Key is encrypted at rest and used only by the Rioko worker to authenticate requests against the Vendus API.
shopifyVendusSetup.step1Desc: Connect your Shopify store using a Custom App Access Token.
shopifyVendusSetup.step2Desc: Enter the Vendus API key and environment.
shopifyVendusSetup.step3Desc: Register ID, series, VAT, finalization and exemption.
shopifyVendusSetup.activateDesc: Confirm and activate the Shopify → Vendus sync.
shopifyVendusSetup.areYouSure: Are you sure? Click to confirm
shopifyVendusSetup.errorShopifyRequired: Fill in Shopify domain and token.
shopifyVendusSetup.errorShopifyInvalid: Could not validate Shopify. Check domain and token.
shopifyVendusSetup.errorSettingsRequired: Fill in Register ID and Series.
shopifyVendusSetup.vatIncludedOff: Adds 23% on top of the price
shopifyVendusSetup.exemptionDesc: Applied when the invoice has 0% VAT (exemption, reverse charge, etc).
shopifyVendusSetup.activateWarning: Once active, Rioko starts processing Shopify webhooks and issuing Vendus documents in real time.

shopifyMoloniSetup.title: Shopify + Moloni
shopifyMoloniSetup.subtitle: Connect your Moloni account to issue invoices automatically when a Shopify order is paid.
shopifyMoloniSetup.limitationsBody: This integration does not yet support VIES / intra-EU reverse-charge (B2B EU) for Moloni — for B2B EU with M16 exemption use Shopify + InvoiceXpress. Portuguese B2C and OSS customers work without issues.
shopifyMoloniSetup.shopifyMissingTitle: Shopify is not connected and authorized yet.
shopifyMoloniSetup.shopifyMissingBody: You need an active Shopify connection before order webhooks can flow through. Configure Shopify+InvoiceXpress (or Shopify alone) first, then come back.
shopifyMoloniSetup.clientIdHint: From the application you created on the Moloni developer panel (www.moloni.pt/dev → My applications).
shopifyMoloniSetup.clientSecretHint: Shown only once when the Moloni application is created. If lost, create a new application.
shopifyMoloniSetup.secretStoredHint: Already stored. Leave blank to keep.
shopifyMoloniSetup.usernameHint: Email you log into moloni.pt with (recommended: a dedicated Rioko user with invoicing-only permissions).
shopifyMoloniSetup.passwordHint: Encrypted at rest. Used only to mint short-lived access tokens.
shopifyMoloniSetup.passwordStoredHint: Already stored. Leave blank to keep.
shopifyMoloniSetup.companyIdHint: In Moloni: Settings → Company. The ID appears at the top.
shopifyMoloniSetup.documentSetIdHint: In Moloni: Settings → Documents → Document Sets. Pick the set where invoices will be issued.
shopifyMoloniSetup.environmentHint: Use Sandbox to test without affecting your live accounting.
shopifyMoloniSetup.errorMissingSecret: Client Secret missing. Paste the value from the Moloni application.
shopifyMoloniSetup.securityFooter: All credentials are encrypted at rest. The Client Secret and password are used only by the Rioko worker to mint short-lived access tokens against the Moloni API — never shared with third parties or exposed to your browser.
shopifyMoloniSetup.productMappingsDesc: Pair Shopify products with existing Moloni products (optional)

stripeMoloniSetup.title: Stripe + Moloni
stripeMoloniSetup.subtitle: Connect your Moloni account to issue invoices automatically when Stripe collects a payment.
stripeMoloniSetup.stripeMissingBody: You need an active Stripe source connection before Moloni receives anything. Configure Stripe first, then come back.
stripeMoloniSetup.clientIdHint: From the application you created on the Moloni developer panel (www.moloni.pt/dev → My applications).
stripeMoloniSetup.clientSecretHint: Shown only once when the Moloni application is created. If lost, create a new application.
stripeMoloniSetup.secretStoredHint: Already stored. Leave blank to keep.
stripeMoloniSetup.usernameHint: Email you log into moloni.pt with (recommended: a dedicated Rioko user with invoicing-only permissions).
stripeMoloniSetup.passwordHint: Encrypted at rest. Used only to mint short-lived access tokens.
stripeMoloniSetup.passwordStoredHint: Already stored. Leave blank to keep.
stripeMoloniSetup.companyIdHint: In Moloni: Settings → Company. The ID appears at the top.
stripeMoloniSetup.documentSetIdHint: In Moloni: Settings → Documents → Document Sets. Pick the set where invoices will be issued.
stripeMoloniSetup.environmentHint: Use Sandbox to test without affecting your live accounting.
stripeMoloniSetup.errorMissingSecret: Client Secret missing. Paste the value from the Moloni application.
stripeMoloniSetup.securityFooter: All credentials are encrypted at rest. The Client Secret and password are used only by the Rioko worker to mint short-lived access tokens against the Moloni API — never shared with third parties or exposed to your browser.
stripeMoloniSetup.step1Desc: Connect your Stripe account — webhook installed automatically.
stripeMoloniSetup.step2Desc: Enter the credentials for your Moloni application.
stripeMoloniSetup.step3Desc: Company, document set, VAT, finalization and exemption.
stripeMoloniSetup.activateDesc: Confirm and activate the Stripe → Moloni sync.
stripeMoloniSetup.areYouSure: Are you sure? Click to confirm
stripeMoloniSetup.disabledBody: The Stripe → Moloni integration is in early access. Contact the administrator to enable NEXT_PUBLIC_STRIPE_SOURCE_ENABLED.
stripeMoloniSetup.restrictedKeyHowTitle: How to create the Restricted Key
stripeMoloniSetup.restrictedKeyStep1: Stripe Dashboard → Developers → API keys → Restricted keys → Create restricted key.
stripeMoloniSetup.restrictedKeyStep2: Required permissions (toggle Read on each): Core, Billing, Connect, Checkout, Orders, Radar, Tax. Plus Webhook Endpoints: Write. Everything else: None.
stripeMoloniSetup.restrictedKeyStep3: If Connected Permissions is available, grant Read to the same: Core, Billing, Checkout, Orders, Radar, Tax.
stripeMoloniSetup.restrictedKeyStep4: Copy the key (starts with rk_live_ or rk_test_) and paste it below. Rioko will create the webhook automatically.
stripeMoloniSetup.manualFallbackBody: Auto-install failed. Create the webhook manually in the Stripe Dashboard using the URL below and paste the Signing Secret.
stripeMoloniSetup.errorMoloniRequired: Fill in Moloni Client ID and username.
stripeMoloniSetup.errorSettingsRequired: Fill in Company ID and Document Set ID.
stripeMoloniSetup.vatIncludedOff: Adds 23% on top of the price
stripeMoloniSetup.exemptionDesc: Applied when the invoice has 0% VAT (exemption, reverse charge, etc).
stripeMoloniSetup.activateWarning: Once active, Rioko starts processing Stripe webhooks and issuing Moloni invoices in real time. You can pause anytime.
stripeMoloniSetup.productMappingsDesc: Pair Stripe products with existing Moloni products (optional)

stripeIxSetup.step1Desc: Connect your Stripe account — webhook installed automatically.
stripeIxSetup.step2Desc: Enter your InvoiceXpress account details to issue invoices.
stripeIxSetup.activateDesc: Confirm and activate the Stripe → InvoiceXpress sync.
stripeIxSetup.disabledBody: The Stripe → InvoiceXpress integration is in early access. Contact the administrator to enable the flag NEXT_PUBLIC_STRIPE_SOURCE_ENABLED.
stripeIxSetup.restrictedKeyHowTitle: How to create the Restricted Key
stripeIxSetup.restrictedKeyStep1: Stripe Dashboard → Developers → API keys → Restricted keys → Create restricted key.
stripeIxSetup.restrictedKeyStep2: Required permissions (toggle Read on each): Core, Billing, Connect, Checkout, Orders, Radar, Tax. Plus Webhook Endpoints: Write. Everything else: None.
stripeIxSetup.restrictedKeyStep3: If Connected Permissions is available, grant Read to the same: Core, Billing, Checkout, Orders, Radar, Tax.
stripeIxSetup.restrictedKeyStep4: Copy the key (starts with rk_live_ or rk_test_) and paste it below. Rioko will create the webhook automatically.
stripeIxSetup.manualFallbackBody: Auto-install failed. Create the webhook manually in the Stripe Dashboard using the URL below and paste the Signing Secret.
stripeIxSetup.vatIncludedOff: Adds 23% on top of the price
stripeIxSetup.retentionOn: ON: Apply {value}% IRS/IRC on every invoice
stripeIxSetup.retentionOff: OFF: No withholding on issued invoices
stripeIxSetup.retentionPercentDesc: Consult your accountant for the right rate
stripeIxSetup.areYouSure: Are you sure? Click to confirm
stripeIxSetup.activateWarning: When you mark it as active, Rioko starts processing Stripe webhooks and issuing InvoiceXpress invoices in real time. You can pause anytime.
stripeIxSetup.confirmSequenceMissing: The invoice series "{name}" was not found in InvoiceXpress. Continue anyway? (Rioko will fall back to the default series)
stripeIxSetup.overridesDesc: Force VAT rate, inclusion (inc/exc) or exemption per SKU
stripeIxSetup.tagRoutingDesc: Route payments to specific IX series or document type based on Stripe metadata

devMode.title: Dev Mode
devMode.noShop: This account has no `shopify_domain` configured. Dev Mode actions unavailable.
devMode.subscriptionAdminDesc: Admins don't need a subscription — integration always runs.
devMode.subscriptionDesc: Controls early bird + trial end date for this user. Doesn't create a Stripe sub — only marks eligibility for trial checkout.
devMode.taxOverrideDesc: Forces a fixed rate on every issued invoice (backfill + webhooks). Leave empty to use Shopify data.
devMode.b2bM40: M40 — Art. 6.º n.º 6 b) (EU B2B services)
devMode.taxOverrideExplain1: applies fixed rate to lines with product_id (books, etc.). Empty = use Shopify.
devMode.taxOverrideExplain2: applies fixed rate to the shipping line (no product_id). Empty = use Shopify (default).
devMode.taxOverrideExplain3: informational for now. Full small-seller rotation requires a builder rewrite.
devMode.taxOverrideExplain4: with OSS active + VAT-inclusive prices (vat_included), EU cross-border orders with Company + VIES-validated VAT ID are issued at 0% VAT + Art. 196 of the VAT Directive note. Without VIES confirmation, the invoice goes pending and is validated manually in the incidents panel.
devMode.viesPendingDesc: Orders with VAT IDs that VIES couldn't confirm. Validate manually at viesvalidation.com/pt and approve (reverse charge) or reject (normal B2C invoice).
devMode.notifyDesc: Recipients automatically included in every Dev Mode job.
devMode.backfillDesc: Generates missing invoices for historical Shopify orders. Checks DB + InvoiceXpress to avoid duplicates.
devMode.dryRunDesc: Dry-run (simulation — does not write to InvoiceXpress)
devMode.reemitDesc: Forces invoice creation for a specific order. Useful to fix one-off failures.
devMode.forceLabel: Force (ignore IX dedup + delete previous record)
devMode.linkIxDesc: Manually attach an InvoiceXpress document to a charge stuck on 'Processing'. Use when the invoice was issued by hand and auto-matching missed it.
devMode.linkIxEmpty: No unlinked charges — all already have an IX invoice.
devMode.stripeRecoveryDesc: Inspect this client's Stripe webhook endpoints, re-enable one Stripe disabled, delete orphan endpoints, and replay missed events.
devMode.stripeNoConn: No Stripe connection with a restricted_key for this user
devMode.stripeConfirmDelete: Delete this Stripe webhook endpoint? This cannot be undone.
devMode.cancelInvoiceDesc: Deletes draft (if not finalized) or issues a credit note (if already finalized), based on order number.
devMode.confirmDeleteDraft: Confirm: delete draft for #{n}? Irreversible in IX.
devMode.confirmCreditNote: Confirm: issue credit note for #{n}? Irreversible in IX.
devMode.finalizeDesc: Iterates processed_orders (oldest→newest), identifies IX drafts, adjusts date per chosen strategy and finalizes.
devMode.stratClosestDesc: Keeps original date if valid; otherwise uses the last finalized date
devMode.stratTodayDesc: Forces all drafts to today's date
devMode.stratNote: When the date must be adjusted, the invoice note gets the suffix Invoice for order #X dated DD/MM/YYYY (preserves existing content).
devMode.filter: Filter (to avoid timeout on large loops)
devMode.logsDesc: History of Dev Mode jobs, errors and webhooks per account.

help.pageSubtitle: Rioko 2.0 · How to get credentials for each platform
help.pageIntro: Pick a platform to see how to get the required credentials. Rioko connects a source (Shopify or Stripe) to an invoicing destination (InvoiceXpress or Moloni). Each platform has its own credentials — open the matching tab.
help.thisStepByKapta: This step is handled by Kapta
help.thisStepByKaptaBody: The Shopify Access Token is generated and installed by the Kapta technical team during initial setup. Never share this token with anyone other than Kapta.
help.shopifyDomainInfo: The store domain is the unique address Shopify assigned when you signed up. It always has the format store-name.myshopify.com.
help.shopifyDomainStep4: Copy the value (without the https:// prefix) and paste it into Rioko's Domain field.
help.shopifyDomainWarn: ⚠️ Even if you have a custom domain (e.g. www.my-store.com), always use the .myshopify.com here. The Shopify API rejects custom domains.
help.shopifyTokenInfo: The Access Token is the key that lets Rioko read orders and install webhooks on your store. For security, it is generated and installed by Kapta — you never copy this value yourself.
help.shopifyTokenWarn: 🔐 If the token is shared by mistake, revoke it immediately in Shopify Admin → Apps → Develop apps and contact us to generate a new one.
help.shopifyTokenOldOrders: Orders older than 60 days — By default, the Admin API only returns recent orders. To bulk re-issue old invoices, the token must include the read_all_orders scope. If you see "Order #XXXX not found" for an old order, ask us to regenerate the token with that scope.
help.shopifyWebhookInfo: Key used by Shopify to sign every webhook it sends to Rioko. It lets us confirm each notification actually came from your store and not from a third party.
help.shopifyWebhookStep3: You'll see the phrase "Your webhooks will be signed with:" followed by a key.
help.shopifyWebhookStep4: Copy that key and paste it into Rioko's Webhook Signing Secret field.
help.shopifyWebhookExtra: Rioko automatically installs the orders/paid, refunds/create and orders/updated webhooks when you click "Activate". If the token lacks webhook permission, Rioko shows a manual install button.
help.shopifyApiVersionInfo: The "API version" field controls which Admin API release to use. Rioko defaults to the latest version (2026-01). Only change if Kapta tells you to.
help.stripeAccountIdInfo: The Account ID identifies your Stripe account (format acct_XXXXXXXXXXXX). Rioko uses it to ensure each incoming webhook belongs to your account.
help.stripeAccountIdStep2: In the top-left, open the account selector (shows the company name).
help.stripeAccountIdStep3: The Account ID appears under the name, starting with acct_.
help.stripeAccountIdStep4: Click the copy icon and paste it into Rioko's Account ID field.
help.stripeRestrictedInfo: We use a Restricted Key instead of the secret key. It only reads what Rioko needs to issue invoices — it can never make payments or refunds on your behalf.
help.stripeRestrictedStep1: In the Stripe dashboard, open Developers → API keys.
help.stripeRestrictedStep4: Grant Read to: Charges, PaymentIntents, Customers, Tax IDs, Balance transactions, Refunds.
help.stripeRestrictedStep6: Click "Create key", copy the value starting with rk_live_ (or rk_test_ in test mode) and paste it into Rioko.
help.stripeRestrictedWarn: 🔐 The key is shown only once. If you lose it, create a new one and revoke the previous in Developers → API keys → Revoke.
help.stripeWebhookInfo: Rioko reacts to every payment received on Stripe. For that, you add an endpoint in Stripe pointing to Rioko and copy the signing secret Stripe gives you.
help.stripeWebhookStep4: Confirm creation. On the endpoint page, click "Reveal" on the Signing secret.
help.stripeWebhookStep5: Copy the value starting with whsec_ and paste it into Rioko's Webhook Secret field.
help.stripeWebhookWarn: ⚠️ The Stripe → Invoicing integration is in pre-launch. If you don't see the setup screen in your dashboard yet, contact us to enable the flag.
help.ixAccountInfo: The InvoiceXpress account name is your account subdomain (e.g. company-name in company-name.app.invoicexpress.com).
help.ixAccountStep2: In the top-right, open your account menu.
help.ixAccountStep5: If you haven't enabled the API, click "Enable".
help.ixAccountStep6: The account name appears in the first field. Copy and paste into Rioko.
help.ixApiKeyInfo: The API Key authenticates Rioko when we create invoices and receipts on your behalf. It's the only sensitive credential for this integration.
help.ixApiKeyStep1: On the same Account Preferences → API screen, copy the value from the API Key field (second field).
help.ixApiKeyStep2: Paste it into the matching Rioko field.
help.ixApiKeyWarn: 🔐 The API Key lets you create, finalize and cancel documents on your account. Don't share it with anyone other than Kapta.
help.ixEnvProdBody: Real invoices, with fiscal value, reported to AT. Use when the store is live.
help.ixEnvSandboxBody: Test environment, no fiscal value. Only for technical validation before go-live.
help.ixDocTypeInfo: Choose whether Rioko issues Invoice-Receipts (recommended for e-commerce — sale and payment at the same time) or simple Invoices (sell now, pay later — useful for B2B with terms).
help.ixDocTypeWarn: ⚠️ If you choose only Invoice, the document stays open until you manually issue the Receipt when the client pays.
help.ixSequenceInfo: Series let you separate documents into numbered "folders" (e.g. WEB series, Storefront series). If left empty, Rioko uses your account's Default series.
help.ixSequenceStep1: Under InvoiceXpress → Settings → State Communication → Invoice Series / ATCUD, identify the series to use.
help.ixSequenceStep2: Write the exact series name in Rioko (case-sensitive).
help.ixSequenceStep3: If the name is wrong, Rioko issues on the Default series to avoid failure.
help.vatInfo: Defines how Rioko interprets prices received from the payment platform. VAT Included (Shopify default in PT): the total price already has VAT added. Rioko subtracts VAT to compute the net amount before sending to InvoiceXpress. VAT Excluded: prices arrive net, VAT is added after. Rule of thumb: if your shop is Portuguese B2C, keep VAT Included (the prices customers see already contain VAT).
help.vatWarn: ⚠️ If you disable when it should be enabled, InvoiceXpress will issue invoices with totals higher than what the customer paid. Always confirm your store's VAT configuration before toggling.
help.autoFinalizeInfo: When an order is paid, Rioko creates the invoice as a draft in InvoiceXpress (state draft). With Auto-finalize on, Rioko automatically transitions to finalized as soon as the payment is confirmed — AT communication is immediate. With it off, the invoice stays as a draft and you must finalize manually in InvoiceXpress → Documents → drafts.
help.autoFinalizeStep1: Enabled (recommended for e-commerce): documents are finalized and communicated to AT within seconds. Fully automatic flow.
help.autoFinalizeStep2: Disabled: documents remain as drafts until you manually approve. Useful in B2B models with prior approval or when issuing pro-forma invoices that may still change.
help.autoFinalizeStep3: You can toggle this at any time — already-finalized documents are unaffected.
help.autoFinalizeWarn: ⚠️ In Portugal, you must finalize and communicate to AT within 5 working days. If you disable, ensure you regularly finalize drafts to meet the legal deadline.
help.retentionInfo: Applies to services provided to Portuguese B2B clients when the issuer is subject to withholding. When enabled, Rioko automatically adds the retention field to all InvoiceXpress invoices with the percentage you set (typically 16.5% or 25% depending on IRS/IRC regime).
help.retentionStep1: When to enable: liberal professionals (doctors, lawyers, consultants) or businesses providing services to other businesses with withholding obligations.
help.retentionStep2: Percentage: choose the rate applicable to your CAE. If unsure, consult your accountant — a wrong rate triggers fiscal corrections.
help.retentionStep3: When NOT to enable: B2C e-commerce of physical goods (no withholding on consumer goods sales).
help.retentionWarn: ⚠️ Withholding changes the final invoice value (customer pays less, you collect the withheld amount via IRS/IRC). Confirm with an accountant before enabling.
help.exemptionInfo:
When an invoice has lines at 0% VAT, AT requires a reason code for exemption. Rioko automatically applies the code you choose to exempt lines. Most common codes:
  • M01 — Article 16(6) of CIVA (intra-EU B2C supply — OSS)
  • M05 — Exempt article 14 of CIVA (exports outside EU)
  • M07 — Exempt article 9 of CIVA (specific services)
  • M16 — Exempt article 14 of RITI (intra-community B2B with valid VAT — reverse charge)
  • M30-M35 — Reverse charge (subject substitution)
  • M40-M43 — VAT self-liquidation
  • M99 — Not subject / not taxed (generic, last resort)
help.exemptionWarn: ⚠️ Using the wrong code is a fiscal infraction. If unsure, consult your accountant. Rioko applies the code you choose to ALL exempt lines, except special cases (intra-community B2B automatically uses M16 when reverse-charge is enabled).
help.moloniDevInfo: Moloni only allows API access through a developer account. It's free and takes 2 minutes to create.
help.moloniDevStep2: Click "Register" and use a different email from your main account (recommended).
help.moloniDevStep3: Confirm the email and sign in to the developer panel.
help.moloniAppInfo: Each integration needs an "application" tied to your account. Moloni provides a Client ID and Client Secret that Rioko uses to authenticate.
help.moloniAppStep1: In the developer panel, open "My applications".
help.moloniAppStep5: Save. You'll see the Client ID and Client Secret in the app details.
help.moloniAppStep6: Copy both into Rioko (Moloni Client ID and Moloni Client Secret fields).
help.moloniAppWarn: 🔐 The Client Secret is shown only once. If you lose it, create a new application and delete the previous one.
help.moloniCredsInfo: Moloni uses the password grant flow: beyond the application credentials, you must provide the username and password of the Moloni account where invoices should be created. Rioko encrypts these credentials at rest and uses them only to mint short-lived access tokens.
help.moloniCredsStep1: Username: the email you sign into moloni.pt with (not the developer one).
help.moloniCredsStep3: Company ID: inside Moloni, go to Settings → Company. The ID appears at the top. Paste into Rioko.
help.moloniCredsWarn: ⚠️ Recommended: create a dedicated Moloni user for Rioko (e.g. rioko@company.com) with invoicing-only permissions. That way you can revoke the integration without touching your personal account.
help.moloniDocTypesInfo: Like in InvoiceXpress, you can choose between Invoice-Receipt (default for e-commerce) and simple Invoice. The choice is made on the integration screen after validating the credentials.
help.moloniDocTypesWarn: ⚠️ The Moloni integration is in final testing. If you don't see the option in your dashboard yet, contact us.
help.footerBody: If you still have questions about configuring Rioko, our team is available to help — by email or scheduled call.
help.faqShopifyQ1: Does Rioko issue invoices at payment time or at order creation?
help.faqShopifyA1: By default, Rioko issues at payment time (Shopify orders/paid event). That way the invoice always matches money that actually came in. Orders created but not paid don't generate a document.
help.faqShopifyQ2: How do I re-issue an invoice for an old order (more than 60 days)?
help.faqShopifyA2: Your Access Token must have the read_all_orders scope. Contact Kapta to regenerate the token with that scope; then use the Reconciliation option in the dashboard to re-issue.
help.faqShopifyQ3: Does Rioko sync changes to an order after payment?
help.faqShopifyA3: Yes — orders/updated events are handled and, if the invoice is still in draft, it's updated. After finalization (auto-finalize) the document is immutable by tax law.
help.faqShopifyQ4: What if a customer requests a refund on Shopify?
help.faqShopifyA4: The refunds/create webhook is intercepted and Rioko automatically issues a Credit Note in the invoicing software, linked to the original invoice.
help.faqShopifyQ5: Can I pause the integration temporarily?
help.faqShopifyA5: Yes. On the integration page, at the top, there's a toggle Integration active / paused. When paused, webhooks keep arriving but no document is issued until you reactivate.
help.faqShopifyQ6: Does the Access Token need periodic renewal?
help.faqShopifyA6: No. Shopify Custom App tokens don't expire — they're only invalidated if revoked manually or if the app is uninstalled.
help.faqStripeQ1: Why does Rioko use a Restricted Key instead of the Secret Key?
help.faqStripeA1: Least-privilege principle. The Restricted Key only allows reads of the needed resources (Charges, PaymentIntents, Customers, Tax IDs, Balance transactions, Refunds). Even if compromised, no one could move money or alter data.
help.faqStripeQ2: What happens if a Stripe payment fails?
help.faqStripeA2: Failed payments (payment_intent.payment_failed) don't generate a document. Only payment_intent.succeeded and charge.succeeded trigger invoice issuance.
help.faqStripeQ3: Can Rioko extract the customer's VAT ID from Stripe?
help.faqStripeA3: Yes, if the customer has Tax IDs attached to the Stripe Customer. The Restricted Key includes read access to that resource. If there's no Tax ID, the invoice is issued as Final Consumer.
help.faqStripeA4: charge.refunded with amount_refunded < amount generates a Credit Note proportional to the refunded amount.
help.faqStripeQ5: Can I have Stripe as the primary source and InvoiceXpress as the destination?
help.faqStripeA5: Yes. Rioko treats each (source, destination) pair as an independent connection. You can run Shopify→IX, Stripe→IX and Stripe→Moloni in parallel on the same account.
help.faqIxQ1: Does Rioko report invoices to the Tax Authority?
help.faqIxA1: Yes, indirectly. InvoiceXpress is integrated with AT and automatically reports every document finalized in Production. Rioko issues the documents and InvoiceXpress handles tax communication.
help.faqIxA2: When on, Rioko finalizes (certifies) every invoice immediately after creation — it's immediately valid and reported to AT. When off, the invoice stays as a draft for manual review in InvoiceXpress.
help.faqIxQ3: How do I set up withholding tax (IRS/IRC)?
help.faqIxA3: In the integration, enable Withholding and set the percentage (0–99.99). Rioko includes the retention field in every document. If you disable it, the percentage is kept so you can re-enable without losing the setup.
help.faqIxQ4: Can I have more than one invoice series?
help.faqIxA4: Yes. To alternate between series, you can edit the Series field in the integration before each batch. For continuous volume, we recommend keeping one dedicated Rioko series.
help.faqIxQ5: What happens if the API Key expires or is revoked?
help.faqIxA5: Rioko starts logging 401 errors and marks the integration as unauthorized. Generate a new key in Account Preferences → API and paste it into Rioko — no further reconfiguration needed.
help.faqMoloniQ1: Does Moloni also report to AT like InvoiceXpress?
help.faqMoloniA1: Yes. Moloni is AT-certified invoicing software and automatically reports every document finalized in Production.
help.faqMoloniQ2: Why do I need a separate Moloni developer account?
help.faqMoloniA2: It's how Moloni organizes API access: the main account holds company data, and the developer account holds applications (Client ID/Secret) that can access it via API. A security split.
help.faqMoloniQ3: Is it safe to store my Moloni password in Rioko?
help.faqMoloniA3: The password is encrypted at rest and used only by Rioko to trade for short-lived access tokens. Even so, we recommend a dedicated Moloni user for Rioko (with invoicing-only permissions) to reduce risk in case of incident.
help.faqMoloniQ4: Can I use Moloni for B2B with intra-EU reverse charge?
help.faqMoloniA4: Yes. When Rioko detects a B2B customer with a valid VAT ID outside Portugal and reverse charge is active in the integration, the document is issued with exemption M16 (Article 14 of RITI) in Moloni.
help.faqMoloniQ5: How long does the Moloni setup take end-to-end?
help.faqMoloniA5: About 10–15 minutes: 2 min to create a developer account, 5 min to create the application and copy credentials, 5 min to validate and run a test issuance.

lodgifyMoloniSetup.noteBody: Each confirmed Lodgify booking generates a Moloni invoice automatically. The webhook is registered directly in Lodgify using your API key — no manual setup needed.
lodgifyMoloniSetup.apiKeyHint: In Lodgify, go to Settings → Public API and copy your API key. The webhook is registered automatically.
lodgifyMoloniSetup.apiKeyStoredHint: API key stored. Enter a new one to reconnect the webhook.
lodgifyMoloniSetup.webhookBody: This URL was automatically registered in your Lodgify account. For reference only.
lodgifyMoloniSetup.webhookManualNote: Auto-registration failed. Add the URL above manually: Lodgify → Settings → Integrations → Webhooks.
lodgifyMoloniSetup.step1Desc: Enter your Lodgify API key. The webhook is auto-registered.
lodgifyMoloniSetup.step2Desc: Enter the credentials for your Moloni application.
lodgifyMoloniSetup.step3Desc: Company, document set, VAT, finalization and exemption.
lodgifyMoloniSetup.activateDesc: Confirm and activate the Lodgify → Moloni sync.
lodgifyMoloniSetup.secretStoredHint: Already stored. Leave blank to keep.
lodgifyMoloniSetup.passwordStoredHint: Already stored. Leave blank to keep.
lodgifyMoloniSetup.companyNameHint: Type the company name exactly as it appears in Moloni. We look up the ID when you save.
lodgifyMoloniSetup.documentSetNameHint: Type the document set name exactly as in Moloni (e.g. FR2026). We look up the ID when you save.
lodgifyMoloniSetup.vatIncludedOff: Adds 23% on top of the price
lodgifyMoloniSetup.exemptionDesc: Applied when the invoice has 0% VAT (exemption, reverse charge, etc).
lodgifyMoloniSetup.productMappingsDesc: Pair Lodgify booking categories with existing Moloni products (optional)
lodgifyMoloniSetup.activateWarning: Once active, Rioko starts processing Lodgify bookings and issuing Moloni invoices in real time. You can pause anytime.
lodgifyMoloniSetup.areYouSure: Are you sure? Click to confirm
lodgifyMoloniSetup.errorMissingApiKey: Lodgify API key is required to connect.
lodgifyMoloniSetup.errorMoloniRequired: Fill in Moloni Client ID and username.
lodgifyMoloniSetup.errorMissingSecret: Client Secret missing. Paste the value from the Moloni application.
lodgifyMoloniSetup.errorSettingsRequired: Fill in Company ID and Document Set ID.

lodgifyVendusSetup.noteBody: Each confirmed Lodgify booking generates a Vendus invoice automatically. The webhook is registered directly in Lodgify using your API key — no manual setup needed.
lodgifyVendusSetup.lodgifyKeyHint: In Lodgify, go to Settings → Public API and copy your API key. The webhook is registered automatically.
lodgifyVendusSetup.lodgifyKeyStoredHint: API key stored. Enter a new one to reconnect the webhook.
lodgifyVendusSetup.webhookBody: This URL was automatically registered in your Lodgify account. For reference only.
lodgifyVendusSetup.webhookManualNote: Auto-registration failed. Add the URL above manually: Lodgify → Settings → Integrations → Webhooks.
lodgifyVendusSetup.step1Desc: Enter your Lodgify API key. The webhook is auto-registered.
lodgifyVendusSetup.step2Desc: Enter the Vendus API key and environment.
lodgifyVendusSetup.step3Desc: Register ID, series, VAT, finalization and exemption.
lodgifyVendusSetup.activateDesc: Confirm and activate the Lodgify → Vendus sync.
lodgifyVendusSetup.apiKeyHint: In the Vendus backoffice, go to Settings → Integrations → Web Services. Generate a dedicated API key for Rioko.
lodgifyVendusSetup.apiKeyStoredHint: Already stored. Leave blank to keep.
lodgifyVendusSetup.registerIdHint: Vendus POS register ID where invoices should be issued.
lodgifyVendusSetup.vatIncludedOff: Adds 23% on top of the price
lodgifyVendusSetup.exemptionDesc: Applied when the invoice has 0% VAT (exemption, reverse charge, etc).
lodgifyVendusSetup.activateWarning: Once active, Rioko starts processing Lodgify bookings and issuing Vendus documents in real time. You can pause anytime.
lodgifyVendusSetup.areYouSure: Are you sure? Click to confirm
lodgifyVendusSetup.errorMissingLodgifyKey: Lodgify API key is required to connect.
lodgifyVendusSetup.errorSettingsRequired: Fill in Register ID and Series.
